title: Server-Survival: Tower Intake
description: Drag a load-balancer tower left and right to catch clean traffic, reject attack packets, and keep your server-survival stack online.

howToPlayJson.title: Server-Survival: Tower Intake
howToPlayJson.objective: Survive a 3-minute traffic storm by catching valid requests and scaling tokens in your tower intake while avoiding bot floods, corrupted packets, and overload traps; post the highest offline-ready leaderboard score before your health or CPU

controls.input: Drag left or right with one thumb along the bottom lane
controls.outcome: Repositions the catcher under falling traffic

controls.input: Tap with second thumb or double-tap
controls.outcome: Consumes one cached scale token to widen the intake for 3 seconds
Closes the intake for a moment so dangerous packets fall past

steps: Green user-request packets and blue cache shards fall from the top of the screen toward your tower intake.
Catch green requests to build combo, and catch blue cache shards to charge Burst Scale.
Avoid red bot packets, black error logs, and fake gold honeypot packets that break combo and damage tower health.
Every 8 seconds the tower enters a readable twist phase called Failover Order: one service color lights up on the tower, and only matching
howToPlayJson.scoring: Score 10 per clean request, 25 per matching Failover request, and 50 per cache shard. Each clean catch raises a combo multiplier up to x5. Catching the wrong packet, missing three clean requests in a row, or taking bot/h
tips: Use short thumb drags instead of chasing every packet; the stream is meant to be read in lanes.
Save Burst Scale for dense surges or Failover Order windows when matching packets are worth more.
Closing the intake for a split second is safer than eating a red bot swarm.
Audio helps: clean catches ping upward in pitch, bot packets buzz, and Failover Order starts with a siren cue.
